title: cycas circinalis subsp. riuminiana
intro: Cycas circinalis subsp. riuminiana, also known as the cycad, is a type of ancient, evergreen coniferous plant native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The seeds of Cycas circinalis subsp. riuminiana contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against cell damage. In traditional herbal medicine, the seeds are often prepared as a tea, powder, or oil, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from colds and flu to skin conditions and wounds.
